metadata.name: measurely-py
metadata.summary: A Python library for interacting with the Measurely API

classes.name: measurely.core.CapturePayload

class_variables.name: measurely.core.CapturePayload.value
documentation: The metric value to be recorded
Define the structure for the metric payload to be sent to the API

classes.name: measurely.core.CaptureResult

class_variables.name: measurely.core.CaptureResult.success
documentation: Indicates whether the API call was successful

class_variables.name: measurely.core.CaptureResult.message
documentation: Contains the server's response or an error message
Define the structure for the result returned from the API after capturing a metric

classes.name: measurely.core.Measurely

methods.name: measurely.core.Measurely.init
documentation:
Initializes the Measurely package with your API key.
This method must be called before using other functions.

methods.name: measurely.core.Measurely.capture
documentation:
Sends a metric to Measurely for tracking.

Parameters:
- metric_identifier: The unique identifier for the metric you are capturing.
- payload: A CapturePayload object containing the metric value.

Returns:
- A CaptureResult object indicating the success or failure of the API call.
The Measurely class provides methods for interacting with the Measurely API
